hotels.name: Amavi Hotel
hotels.why: Adults-only resort with a long beach strip, all-day à la carte dining, and easy access to the Akamas Peninsula.

hotels.name: Almyra Hotel
hotels.why: Design-forward seafront hotel. The pool-bar view of Paphos Castle at sunset is the best in town.

hotels.name: Annabelle Hotel
hotels.why: Full-service beach hotel with multiple pools and walking distance to the Archaeological Park.

activities.name: Paphos Archaeological Park
activities.note: UNESCO site with 4th-century BC mosaics of Dionysus. Go early before tour buses arrive.

activities.name: Rock of Aphrodite
activities.note: The legendary birthplace of Aphrodite. Short coastal walk with turquoise water below the cliffs.

activities.name: Akamas Peninsula Hike
activities.note: 7.5 km loop through protected wilderness. Bring water — no shade after the first kilometre.

activities.name: Paphos Harbour & Castle
activities.note: Byzantine-era castle at the harbour entrance. Best at golden hour for photos.

activities.name: Tombs of the Kings
activities.note: Underground rock-cut tombs dating to the 4th century BC. Eerie and impressive scale.

restaurants.name: 7 St. Georges Tavern
restaurants.description: Order the meze — 20+ small dishes for €18. Outdoor terrace overlooking the old town.

restaurants.name: Theo's Seafood Restaurant
restaurants.description: No menu, just the catch of the day priced by weight. Ask for the grilled octopus regardless.

restaurants.name: Pralina Café
restaurants.description: Local favourite for halloumi omelettes and fresh-squeezed orange juice.

restaurants.name: Makedonia Souvlaki
restaurants.description: The best souvlaki pittas in Paphos. €2.50. Eat standing at the counter.

restaurants.name: Café Bar Akamantis
restaurants.description: Quiet courtyard café in the old town. Good Cypriot coffee and free Wi-Fi.

slots.activity: Check in, walk the harbour front, coffee at Pralina Café
Paphos Archaeological Park mosaics (get there before 1 pm heat)
Sunset at the Harbour Castle, dinner at Theo's Seafood
Rock of Aphrodite — drive 30 min east on the coastal road
Tombs of the Kings, then Coral Bay beach swim
Meze dinner at 7 St. Georges Tavern
Akamas Peninsula hike — start by 8 am, bring 2 L water per person
Recover at hotel pool or Lara Bay turtle beach
Souvlaki at Makedonia, walk the harbourfront

practical.sim: Cytamobile-Vodafone eSIM via their app. 10 GB for €10. Works island-wide.
practical.transport: Car hire strongly recommended — €25–40/day from PFO arrivals hall. Bolt operates in Paphos town only.

hotels.name: Radisson Blu Hotel Larnaka
hotels.why: Right on the Finikoudes promenade. Walk to everything — the castle, the salt lake, the best restaurants.

hotels.name: Sun Hall Hotel
hotels.why: Classic seafront property with a rooftop pool. Great value and central location.

activities.name: Hala Sultan Tekke Mosque
activities.note: One of the most important Islamic sites in the world, set beside the salt lake. Serene.

activities.name: Larnaka Salt Lake & Flamingos
activities.note: Flamingos arrive November to March. Off-season it's still a striking landscape.

activities.name: Larnaka Medieval Castle
activities.note: Ottoman-era castle right on the beach. Small but worth it for the views from the roof.

activities.name: Choirokoitia Neolithic Settlement
activities.note: UNESCO site 30 min west. One of the oldest towns in Europe, dating to 7000 BC.

restaurants.name: Art Café 1900
restaurants.description: Restored traditional house. Best mezedes in Larnaka, book ahead at weekends.

restaurants.name: Militzis Restaurant
restaurants.description: Family-run tavern with live Cypriot music on weekends. Try the lamb kleftiko.

restaurants.name: Zephyros Café
restaurants.description: Seafront café with fresh pastries and strong coffee. Perfect starting point for the day.

restaurants.name: Stou Rousha
restaurants.description: The best souvlaki in Larnaka. Look for the queue — it's always there.

slots.activity: Walk the Finikoudes promenade, breakfast at Zephyros
Hala Sultan Tekke and Salt Lake walk
Larnaka Castle at dusk, dinner at Militzis
Choirokoitia Neolithic Settlement — 30 min drive west
Back in town: Larnaka Medieval Castle + beach swim

practical.sim: PrimeTel or Cyta eSIM available online. 5 GB for €8. Full island coverage.
practical.transport: Taxis from LCA airport are metered and reasonable. Car hire available if you plan day trips.
